title: Library
surface: Archive + topical navigation, depends on thesis
summary: v2's secondary discovery surface. Thesis A: replaces v1's broken tag-detail + archive-tags. Thesis B: also subordinates the topical taxonomy to chronological Dispatches.

strategy.decision: One axis primary, others secondary per Rule 4. Thesis A: topical (tag-grouped, with v1 tag-detail F1 fix via series-style chunking). Thesis B: chronological (Dispatch #N descending) with topical + format filters as secondary. In both theses, tag-detail-as-primary-discovery is rejected per v1 F1 (44k px scrolls).
strategy.why: v1 has two competing archive surfaces — tag-detail (broken at scale) and archive-tags index (working but underused). Rule 4 collapses to one. Per competitive synthesis: Simon Willison's 30/page pagination + related-tags sidebar is the closest peer pattern; Stratechery's topic-taxonomy and Pragmatic Engineer's no-tags-just-newsletter are the divergent options. v2 picks per D1.
strategy.shipped: v1: /blog (chronological feed), /blog/tags (alphabetical tag index), /blog/tags/[tag] (broken at scale per F1), /blog/whitepapers and /blog/series as separate routes. Three or four competing archive surfaces.
strategy.gap: v2 Library is one surface with one primary axis. Tag-as-filter survives as secondary navigation; tag-as-detail-page is replaced by the chunking pattern from series-detail (numbered-card group, paginated if >30 items).
strategy.question: D1 picks primary axis. D3 (format-mix specifics) determines whether presentations, fiction, tutorials surface here or are pruned. D8 may rename tags to reader-facing label.

inheritsFromV1: tags.ts canonical-tag list (single source of truth)
breaksFromV1: one Library surface replacing tag-detail + archive-tags + /blog feed split
broken-at-scale tag-detail pattern replaced with series-style chunking

currentState.summary: v1 tag-detail: alphabetical tag index works; per-tag pages break at scale (44k px for /tags/architecture with 44 posts). Three or four competing archive surfaces.
currentState.annotation: v2 collapses to one Library surface. v1 F1 (broken tag-detail) is resolved by replacing the unbroken-list pattern with the series-style numbered-card chunking that's already working in v1.
